SEARCH
Package sttrj.screen

Class Section

Object
Section
public class Section extends Object
A section represents a chunk of the screen with a set width and height.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    Section(int width, int height)
    Constructs a Section instance with the given width and height.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    addLine(String content)
    Adds a given string to a section, wrapping it in a new Line class internally with sensible defaults, if a given String is too long, it will be truncated to fit.
    void
    addLine(Line line)
    Add a given Line to this Section.
    Dimensions
    getDimensions()
    Returns the total Dimensions of a section.
    Line
    getLine(int line)
    Returns the string for a given line
    List<Line>
    getLines(int startLine, int endLine)
    Returns all lines between a starting and ending line inclusive of the start and end.

    Methods inherited from class Object

    clone, equals, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

  • Constructor Details

    • Section

      public Section(int width, int height)
      Constructs a Section instance with the given width and height.
      Parameters:
      width - width
      height - height

  • Method Details

    • addLine

      public void addLine(String content)
      Adds a given string to a section, wrapping it in a new Line class internally with sensible defaults, if a given String is too long, it will be truncated to fit.
      Parameters:
      content - line to add

    • addLine

      public void addLine(Line line)
      Add a given Line to this Section.
      Parameters:
      line - line to add

    • getLine

      public Line getLine(int line)
      Returns the string for a given line
      Parameters:
      line - the line index we want to retrieve
      Returns:
      line at the given index

    • getDimensions

      public Dimensions getDimensions()
      Returns the total Dimensions of a section.
      Returns:
      the Dimensions of a section;

    • getLines

      public List<Line> getLines(int startLine, int endLine)
      Returns all lines between a starting and ending line inclusive of the start and end.
      Parameters:
      startLine - line to start at.
      endLine - line to end on.
      Returns:
      the list of lines.
